name: Tembleque and El Romeral Windmills Trail
name_original: Tembleque y molinos de viento El Romeral – circular desde Villacañas
description: Cycle the 25.9-mile Tembleque and El Romeral Windmills Trail, a moderate mountain biking route through historic Castilla-La Mancha.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails Villacañas traverse diverse landscapes, including significant wetland systems and agricultural areas. The region features flat, open terrain with occasional gentle ascents, characteristic of the La Mancha plains. Routes often follow former railway lines, such as the Vía Verde del Trenillo, and pass through vineyards and areas of Mediterranean steppe. Best mountain bike trails around Villacañas The most popular mountain bike trail is Tembleque and El Romeral Windmills Trail, a 25.9 miles (41.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 21 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the iconic windmills of the region. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Hermitage of San Gregorio – Old train station loop from Villacañas, a moderate 24.9 miles (40.2 km) path. This route follows a former railway line, offering a scenic and relatively flat ride. Local mountain bikers also love the Windmills of El Romeral – El Romeral Windmills loop from Villacañas, a 20.9 miles (33.7 km) trail leading through the open plains and past windmills, often completed in about 2 hours 35 minutes. Mountain biking around Villacañas is defined by the La Mancha Wetlands, extensive vineyards, and the Vía Verde del Trenillo. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy to more challenging moderate routes. The routes in Villacañas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 80 reviews. More than 200 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Villacañas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Villacañas?
A: Mountain bike trails in Villacañas primarily feature flat, open terrain with occasional gentle ascents, characteristic of the La Mancha plains. Routes often follow former railway lines, such as the Vía Verde del Trenillo, and pass through picturesque vineyards and areas of Mediterranean steppe. You'll also encounter significant wetland systems, offering diverse landscapes.

Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Villacañas?
A: There are over 45 mountain bike trails available around Villacañas. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy to more challenging moderate routes.

Q: What is the typical difficulty level of mountain bike trails in Villacañas?
A: The majority of mountain bike trails in Villacañas are classified as moderate, making them suitable for riders with some experience. There are also a few easy routes for beginners and a couple of more difficult options for experienced riders seeking a challenge.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while mountain biking in Villacañas?
A: Villacañas is known for its significant wetland systems, including the &lt;a href="https://fundacionglobalnature.org/la-laguna-larga-de-villacanas-recuperando-un-oasis-natural/" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna Larga de Villacañas&lt;/a&gt;, which is part of the Natural Path of the La Mancha Wetlands. Many routes also pass through vineyards and areas of Mediterranean steppe. You can also explore the iconic windmills of the region, for example, on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025851883/tembleque-and-el-romeral-windmills-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tembleque and El Romeral Windmills Trail&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any specific points of interest along the trails?
A: Yes, several trails offer access to notable points of interest. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4529824" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna Larga of Villacañas&lt;/a&gt;, a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5331427" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Flamingo Observatory&lt;/a&gt; for birdwatching. Other nearby lagoons like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5421216" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna Grande de Villafranca&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3042078" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna Grande de Quero&lt;/a&gt; are also accessible.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy the most about mountain biking in Villacañas?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 80 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscapes, the scenic routes through wetlands and vineyards, and the well-maintained paths, especially those following former railway lines like the Vía Verde del Trenillo.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Villacañas?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes in Villacañas are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38184988" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of San Gregorio – Old train station loop from Villacañas&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20134495" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Windmills of El Romeral – El Romeral Windmills loop from Villacañas&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Villacañas?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' designations are not always explicit, the prevalence of easy and moderate trails, particularly those following the relatively flat Vía Verde del Trenillo, makes many routes suitable for families. These paths often avoid steep climbs and technical sections.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Villacañas?
A: Villacañas offers good mountain biking conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide the most pleasant temperatures. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended during those months. Winters are typically mild, allowing for year-round cycling, though some trails might be affected by rain.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails in Villacañas?
A: Many outdoor trails in Spain, including those in natural areas like the La Mancha Wetlands, are generally dog-friendly, especially if dogs are kept on a leash. However, it's always advisable to check local regulations or specific trail signage for any restrictions, particularly in protected wetland areas to ensure the safety of local wildlife.

Q: Are there options for longer mountain bike tours in Villacañas?
A: Yes, Villacañas offers several longer routes for those looking for extended rides. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31907174" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of San Gregorio – Taray Wetlands loop from Villacañas&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 52.3 km trail, providing a substantial ride through diverse landscapes.

Q: Where can I find parking for mountain biking in Villacañas?
A: While specific parking locations for every trailhead are not detailed, Villacañas is a town with various access points to the surrounding trails. It is generally possible to find parking in or near the town center, or at designated points close to popular trail starts, especially along the Vía Verde del Trenillo.
tips: Another building of great architectural value, the baroque-style palace was built in the 18th century and since 2021 work has been done on it to convert it into a guest house.

Typically La Mancha building, with a square floor plan with a porch of granite columns and corridors on the upper floor, with supports and ornamentation made of wood following the orientations of popular constructions from the 17th century. Designed to fulfill a double function, the pure urban planning and center of life of the population and that of a bullring, so the corridors on its two upper levels are open. On one side is the Town Hall, built in 1654.
A very important heritage element in El Romeral are its four windmills (Muela, Gorrinos, Crítica and Pechuga).

faqs.question: How long does it take to mountain bike the Tembleque and El Romeral Windmills Trail?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for completing this mountain bike trail is approximately 3 hours and 21 minutes. This is based on an average riding pace for the 41.7 km distance.

faqs.question: Is the Tembleque and El Romeral Windmills Trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This trail is classified as 'moderate' and requires good fitness and advanced riding skills. It corresponds to an S2 rating on the Single Trail Scale, meaning you can expect larger roots and rocks, flat steps, and occasionally loose ground. Gradients can reach up to 70%, so it's not recommended for beginners.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The route features diverse terrain, with a significant portion (34.8 km) on natural surfaces. You'll also encounter shorter sections of asphalt (3.25 km), unpaved roads (2.18 km), paved surfaces (1.55 km), and a small segment of singletrack (755 m). Expect roots, rocks, and potentially loose ground in some areas.

faqs.question: What can I see along the Tembleque and El Romeral Windmills Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers a rich blend of cultural and historical sights. You'll pass by the iconic El Romeral Windmills, reminiscent of Don Quixote's adventures. Additionally, the route goes through Tembleque, where you can see the historic Plaza Mayor of Tembleque and the baroque Palacio de las Torres.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to ride this trail is typically during the spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) months. During these periods, the weather in Castilla-La Mancha is generally milder, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the colder, potentially wetter conditions of winter.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit to mountain bike the Tembleque and El Romeral Windmills Trail?
faqs.answer: There is no specific permit required to mountain bike this trail. However, always respect local regulations and private property, and ensure you are riding on designated paths.

faqs.question: Is the Tembleque and El Romeral Windmills Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While there are no explicit restrictions against dogs, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through towns or near livestock. Be mindful of the moderate difficulty and length of the trail, as it might be challenging for some dogs.

faqs.question: Where does the Tembleque and El Romeral Windmills Trail start, and is there parking?
faqs.answer: This is a circular route that starts from Villacañas. You should be able to find parking within Villacañas, as it's a common starting point for local routes. Specific parking areas are not detailed, but public parking is generally available in Spanish towns.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Tembleque and El Romeral Windmills Trail is a circular route, meaning it starts and ends in the same location, Villacañas, without needing to retrace your path.

faqs.question: Does the trail connect with other routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. Notable sections include the Camino de Santiago del Sureste, Camino Alto de El Romeral a Villacañas, and Camino de Ancos, among others.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: The scenery is characteristic of Castilla-La Mancha, featuring wide plains and open landscapes. The most distinctive feature is the presence of the historic windmills at El Romeral, which stand out against the vast, open terrain, offering views typical of central Spain.
